0.q: SpAHA1 generated an increased proton gradient that stimulated the Na+/H+ exchange activity of SpSOS1. In this scenario, more Na+ ions were transported out of cells.
SpAHA1 produced an additional proton gradient and, thus, promoted SpSOS1-mediated Na+ extrusion in Arabidopsis plants co-expressing both the SpAHA1 and SpSOS1 genes.
SpAHA1 generated an increased proton gradient that stimulated the Na+/H+ exchange activity of SpSOS1 in transgenic yeast cells.

1.q: lignin contents in leaves of transgenic lines under salt stress were markedly higher than those of WT, suggesting that SpC3H enhances salt tolerance in A. thaliana by promoting lignin biosynthesis.

2.q: POD and SOD activities in overexpression lines were markedly elevated compared with WT under salt stress, supporting the conclusion that SpC3H enhances salt tolerance by activating the antioxidant enzyme system to eliminate ROS.

5.q: Overexpression of SpAHA1 could alleviate the accumulation of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) induced by NaCl treatment in transgenic plants relative to wild-type plants; the content of malondialdehyde (MDA) was lower in transgenic plants than that in wild-type plants under salinity stress.

7.q: we cloned the SpJAZ1, SpJAZ5 and SpJAZ7 genes... which are involved in JA signaling responses to salt and Cd stress in S. portulacastrum.

8.q: we cloned the SpJAZ1, SpJAZ5 and SpJAZ7 genes... which are involved in JA signaling responses to salt and Cd stress in S. portulacastrum.

9.q: Transformed plants with SpAHA1 had lower Na+ and higher K+ contents relative to wild-type plants when treated with NaCl, resulting in greater K+/Na+ ratio in transgenic plants than in wild-type plants under salt stress.
SpSOS1-expressing roots had faster Na+ efflux relative to WT plants in saline conditions, suggesting the extra Na+ extrusion may be mediated by SpSOS1.
Compared with untransformed yeast cells, SpSOS1 increased the salt tolerance of transgenic yeast by decreasing the Na+ content.

10.q: SpSOS1-expressing roots had faster Na+ efflux relative to WT plants in saline conditions, suggesting the extra Na+ extrusion may be mediated by SpSOS1.
Compared with untransformed yeast cells, SpSOS1 increased the salt tolerance of transgenic yeast by decreasing the Na+ content.

11.q: Transgenic plants demonstrated significantly lower levels of O2- and H2O2 compared to wild-type plants... SpCIPK2 enhances salt tolerance by... activating antioxidant enzymes activity, and scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS) in salt-stressed plants.

12.q: SpSOS1 increased the salt tolerance of transgenic yeast by decreasing the Na+ content. The Na+/H+ exchange activity at plasma membrane vesicles was higher in SpSOS1-transgenic yeast than in the untransformed strain.

13.q: p-coumarate 3-hydroxylase (C3H, belonging to the CYP98A subfamily)... catalyzes hydroxylation at the C3 position, converting p-coumaroyl shikimate/quinate to caffeoyl shikimate/quinate
